title: Jumper and cable management systems and associated hardware
abstract: A rack for mounting electronic equipment, the rack comprises a pair of vertical support members for supporting at least one electronic component, wherein each of the vertical support members includes a regular pattern of mounting apertures that repeats linearly at a first regular interval. The rack also includes a cable management system disposed generally adjacent one of the vertical support members. The cable management system includes a trough, and a plurality of outwardly extending projections forming a plurality of cable rings, wherein each projection of the plurality of outwardly extending projections is configured to interlock with an adjacent projection of the plurality of outwardly extending projections.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A rack for mounting electronic equipment, the rack comprising: a pair of vertical support members for supporting at least one electronic component, wherein each of the vertical support members includes a regular pattern of mounting apertures that repeats linearly at a first regular interval; and a cable management system disposed generally adjacent one of the vertical support members, the cable management system including: a trough; and a plurality of outwardly extending projections forming a plurality of cable rings, wherein each projection of the plurality of outwardly extending projections is configured to interlock with an adjacent projection of the plurality of outwardly extending projections. 2. The rack of claim 1, wherein the cable rings are disposed at a second regular interval along the length of the cable management system, and wherein the second regular interval is a whole number multiple of the first regular interval. 3. The rack of claim 2, wherein each instance of the pattern of mounting apertures includes a plurality of mounting apertures. 4. The rack of claim 3, wherein the first regular interval corresponds to a whole number of standard rack mounting units. 5. The rack of claim 4, wherein the first regular interval is a single standard rack mounting unit. 6. The rack of claim 2, wherein each cable ring is disposed adjacent to a single instance of the pattern of mounting apertures and aligned therewith. 7. The rack of claim 2, wherein the second regular interval corresponds to a whole number of standard rack mounting units.
